keyRules: Maximum 120 m above ground level (EASA Open Category)
Registration via the D-Flight portal: Basic subscription €7/year (PRO €28/year) + mandatory QR code on each drone (Base €7/UAS; the first one is included with the initial subscription)
Mandatory A1/A3 Online Training — Minimum age: 16
Mandatory liability insurance — minimum 750,000 SDR (Special Drawing Rights)
Rome and Vatican City: Extensive No-Fly Zones
Always keep the drone within visual line of sight (VLOS)
Do not fly over assemblies of people. Rules for uninvolved persons depend on subcategory, class mark and operating conditions; check the official matrix.
A minimum distance of 5 km from any airport or airfield
Night flight is permitted under the EASA open-category baseline when the required green flashing light is used, unless a current geographic zone or national rule imposes a stricter condition.
Historic sites and archaeological areas may be subject to additional restrictions
importantNotes: D-Flight Subscription: Basic €7/year (PRO €28/year for a specific category). The first Basic QR Code is included with initial activation; additional Basic QR Codes cost €7 per UAS (PRO €112 per UAS).
Mandatory insurance with a minimum coverage of 750,000 SDRs (Special Drawing Rights)
Rome/Vatican has one of the most restrictive airspaces in Europe for drones
Fines of up to €100,000 for serious violations
The GDPR applies when identifiable images are captured

fields.disclaimerText: Always consult the official sources from ENAC and D-Flight before flying. This app is a tool, not a legal authority. Italy applies EASA regulations with national additions.
